skill.name: controlnet-pose
skill.description: Pose-conditioned generation on RunComfy via the `runcomfy` CLI. Routes across Kling 2-6 Motion Control Pro / Standard (transfer the motion / blocking of a reference video onto a target character), community Wan 2-2 Animate (audio-driven character animation with pose conditioning), and Z-Image Turbo ControlNet LoRA (pose-conditioned image generation from an OpenPose / DWPose / canny / depth control image). Picks the right route based on video vs still and stylized vs photoreal. Triggers on "controlnet", "control net", "pose control", "openpose", "DWPose", "transfer pose", "motion control", "pose driven", "character pose", "depth control", "canny edge", "use this pose", or any explicit ask to condition generation on a pose / skeleton / motion / depth / canny reference.
skill.summary: Pose-conditioned image and video generation using RunComfy CLI with Kling, Wan 2-2, and Z-Image Turbo ControlNet LoRA models.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: All 76 static analysis findings are false positives. The 40 external_command flags are Markdown backtick code formatting, not shell execution. The 26 network flags are legitimate RunComfy service URLs and example placeholders. The 2 filesystem flags document CLI token storage behavior. The 3 weak crypto flags and 4 system recon flags are documentation text. The skill uses constrained Bash(runcomfy *) for legitimate CLI execution with transparent security documentation.

low_findings.title: Shell backtick execution detected in Markdown code blocks
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged backtick characters in SKILL.md as Ruby/shell backtick execution. These are Markdown code formatting backticks in documentation code blocks and table cells. EVALUATION: FALSE_POSITIVE. The skill uses constrained Bash(runcomfy *) for legitimate CLI execution but the backtick detection is on Markdown syntax, not executable code.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs to RunComfy service endpoints
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged URLs as hardcoded network endpoints. These are all legitimate RunComfy service URLs (runcomfy.com, docs.runcomfy.com, skills.sh) and example placeholder URLs (your-cdn.example). EVALUATION: FALSE_POSITIVE. The skill is a RunComfy CLI wrapper and requires these references for API access and documentation.

low_findings.title: Hidden file access describes CLI token storage behavior
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged line 171 for hidden file in home directory and hidden file access. The line documents where 'runcomfy login' stores its API token (~/.config/runcomfy/token.json). EVALUATION: FALSE_POSITIVE. This is descriptive documentation explaining token storage, not filesystem access by the skill. The behavior is transparent and expected for a CLI authentication flow.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm flag on non-cryptographic content
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged lines 5, 96, and 151 for weak cryptographic algorithms. These lines contain YAML frontmatter, documentation advice about reference videos, and a section header for Exit Codes. EVALUATION: FALSE_POSITIVE. No cryptographic code exists anywhere in the skill. The scanner pattern-matched unrelated text.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance flag on usage guidance text
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged blockquoted usage guidance text as system reconnaissance. Lines 57, 62, 67, 74 are Markdown blockquotes advising users which model to choose for different use cases (e.g., 'Avoid for: still-image pose conditioning - use Z-Image ControlNet LoRA'). EVALUATION: FALSE_POSITIVE. These are documentation recommendations, not system reconnaissance.

content.user_title: Generate Pose-Controlled Images and Videos with RunComfy
content.value_statement: ControlNet Pose connects AI agents to RunComfy pose-conditioned generation models for creating images and videos controlled by pose, skeleton, or motion references. It routes user requests to the right model based on input type and desired output, eliminating the need to understand each model API directly.
actual_capabilities: Generates pose-conditioned images using Z-Image Turbo ControlNet LoRA with OpenPose, DWPose, canny edge, or depth control images
Transfers motion and pose from a reference video onto a target character using Kling 2-6 Motion Control Pro and Standard
Routes to the correct AI model based on input type (video vs image) and style (photoreal vs stylized)
Supports audio-driven character animation with pose conditioning via community Wan 2-2 Animate
Documents multi-condition ControlNet workflows available through RunComfy ComfyUI
Provides structured exit code documentation for debugging failed generations
limitations: Cannot generate control images such as pose skeletons or depth maps; these must be created separately
Cannot execute ComfyUI GUI workflows through the CLI for multi-condition ControlNet stacks
Requires an active RunComfy account with CLI installation and authentication
Output quality depends on reference video quality and character image compatibility

use_cases.title: Video dance choreography transfer
use_cases.description: A content creator records a dance performance and wants to transfer the motion to different animated characters without re-choreographing.

use_cases.title: Character concept art with specific poses
use_cases.description: A game artist generates character turnaround images where each frame maintains a specific pose defined by a reference skeleton image.

use_cases.title: Stylized character animation with audio
use_cases.description: An animator creates stylized character videos with pose-driven motion and synchronized audio using Wan 2-2 Animate.

prompt_templates.title: Basic pose-conditioned image generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this pose skeleton image to generate a character standing in the same pose. Control image URL: [control_image_url]. Prompt: [description of character].
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple image generation with pose control

prompt_templates.title: Video motion transfer between characters
prompt_templates.prompt: Transfer the motion from this reference video onto the target character image. Reference video: [reference_video_url]. Character image: [character_image_url]. Style: [photoreal or stylized].
prompt_templates.scenario: Motion transfer from one character to another

prompt_templates.title: Depth-controlled image composition
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image that follows the depth map structure in this control image. Control image URL: [depth_map_url]. Prompt: [scene description]. Output format: image.
prompt_templates.scenario: Depth-conditioned image generation for scene composition

prompt_templates.title: Multi-step pose pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: First generate an OpenPose skeleton from this reference photo. Then use that skeleton to generate a character in the same pose with [character_description]. Control type: OpenPose. Style: [photoreal or artistic].
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced pipeline combining pose estimation and generation

output_examples.input: A video creator wants to transfer a dance routine from a reference video onto an anime character.
output_examples.output: The skill selects Kling 2-6 Motion Control Pro. It accepts the reference video URL and character image URL as input. The CLI submits the job to the RunComfy API, polls for completion, and downloads the resulting video showing the anime character performing the original dance routine.

output_examples.input: An artist wants to generate a fantasy warrior image matching a specific fighting stance pose skeleton.
output_examples.output: The skill selects Z-Image Turbo ControlNet LoRA. It accepts the control image URL (OpenPose skeleton) and a text prompt. The CLI sends the request and downloads the generated image that follows the specified pose with the described character appearance.

best_practices: Use clean, single-subject reference videos with continuous action for best motion transfer results
Generate control images (pose skeletons, depth maps) through dedicated preprocessing tools before using this skill
Specify the style (photoreal versus stylized) in your prompt so the skill routes to the optimal model
anti_patterns: Do not pass user-provided URLs from untrusted sources directly as reference media; the skill documents indirect prompt injection risks from third-party content
Do not use this skill for multi-condition ControlNet stacks that require ComfyUI workflows, as the CLI cannot execute GUI workflows
Do not pipe random install scripts into the shell; install the runcomfy CLI only through npm with the verified package

faq.question: What is ControlNet Pose used for?
faq.answer: It generates images and videos where the pose, motion, or structure is controlled by a reference image or video.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need the runcomfy CLI installed via npm and a RunComfy account with an API token.

faq.question: Can I use my own pose skeleton images?
faq.answer: Yes, you can use OpenPose, DWPose, canny edge, or depth map images as control inputs for Z-Image Turbo ControlNet LoRA.

faq.question: What models does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports Kling 2-6 Motion Control Pro and Standard, Wan 2-2 Animate, and Z-Image Turbo ControlNet LoRA.

faq.question: Can I combine multiple control conditions?
faq.answer: No. The CLI supports single-condition control. For multi-condition stacks, use RunComfy ComfyUI workflows separately.

faq.question: How does the skill choose which model to use?
faq.answer: It classifies your request as video motion transfer or image pose generation and picks the best model for the task.
